_patch_meta.description: MTI status assignments for 102 no-verse terms, 6 Phase 2 flags, and registry status update.

operations.description: H0157G a.hev to love: lover — xref to desire registry

set.status_note: Session B v3.0. Primary love verb (207 occurrences). Cross-registry decision: primary analysis held in desire registry (reg 43). Phase 2 review required before Session D synthesis to confirm adequacy of desire-registry treatment for love-context conclusions. See PH2-103-001.

operations.description: H0157H a.hev to love: friend — xref to desire registry

set.status_note: Session B v3.0. Friend sub-gloss of a.hev verb (13 occurrences). Cross-registry decision: held in desire registry (reg 43). Companion to H0157G.

Session B v3.0. 33 occurrences (0 verses in export — volume limitation). Primary beloved-designation in Song of Songs and OT prophetic material. Note sub-gloss overlap with H1730I (uncle/male-relative): verse set contains both. Love-relevant verses require separation from kinship-usage verses in Phase 2.

Session B v3.0. 13 occurrences (0 verses in export). Divine attribute adjective — used almost exclusively in the divine self-description formula (Exo 34:6 Thirteen Attributes: gracious and compassionate). Central to the love vocabulary as a divine character term. Volume limitation flagged.

Session B v3.0. 191 occurrences (0 verses in export). The object of the love-your-neighbour commandment (Lev 19:18; Mat 22:39; Mar 12:31; Luk 10:27-37). Analytically essential for the love-neighbour dimension. Volume limitation — full verse set required for Session D synthesis.

Session B v3.0. 17 occurrences (0 verses in export). Noun form of sanē (H8130). Antonym cluster companion. 2Sa 13:15 — the hatred with which Amnon hated Tamar was greater than the love with which he had loved her — uses this word as the direct noun-form parallel to ahavah.

Session B v3.0. 101 occurrences (0 verses in export — volume limitation). Friend as the primary object and expression of phileō love. Joh 15:13-15 — Jesus calls his disciples friends, not servants. Analytically significant for the love-friendship-agape relationship. Full verse coverage required for Session D.

Session B v3.0. 10 occurrences (0 verses). Friendship as a form of love. Jam 4:4 — friendship with the world as enmity with God — uses this word: friendship as a defining relational orientation with moral stakes.

Session B v3.0. 9 occurrences (0 verses). Kiss as physical expression of love and greeting. Includes Rom 16:16 (holy kiss), 1Pe 5:14 (kiss of love), and the betrayal kiss context. The kiss is the phileō vocabulary's most bodily expression.

Session B v3.0. 21 occurrences (0 verses). Intensified kiss verb. Includes: Judas's betrayal kiss (Mat 26:48; Mk 14:44); the sinful woman kissing Jesus's feet (Lk 7:38, 45); the father running and kissing the returning prodigal son (Lk 15:20). Analytically significant: the same word used for betrayal and homecoming.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: primary love verb held in desire registry

record.description: H0157G a.hev (to love, 207 occurrences) is a XREF term — primary analysis held in desire registry (reg 43). Before Session D love-vocabulary synthesis, review the desire-registry a.hev analysis to confirm it is adequate for love-context conclusions. If not, supplementary analysis is required within this registry.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: H7356B rachamim verse contamination

record.description: H7356B rachamim has 69 exported verses but a significant portion are womb-usage verses (rechem: H7358 — physical womb in birth/fertility contexts) that entered via the racham root family bleed. Love-specific compassion verses number approximately 35-40. Before synthesis conclusions are drawn about the scale of the compassion vocabulary, the verse set should be cleaned to isolate compassion-specific occurrences.

1 Jo 4:18 — there is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear — is the most analytically precise cross-registry claim in the programme. Requires dedicated cross-registry analysis with the fear registry (reg 61): what kind of fear is displaced by perfect love? Does the OT vocabulary support this structural opposition? This is a Session D priority and potentially one of the most significant structural findings available from the dataset.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: God is love — ontological claim

record.description: 1 Jo 4:8, 16 — God is love (ho theos agapē estin) — is the most radical ontological claim made about the divine nature in the NT. It requires dedicated Session D treatment: how does this claim relate to all other inner-being attributes attributed to God (anger, grief, compassion)? Does this claim govern all other divine emotional attributions? This is the theological summit of the entire programme.

G0025 agapaō has 344 occurrences but only 130 verses in the export. Priority additional coverage: full Pauline love-ethics (Eph 5; 1 Thes 4; Col 3; Gal 5); full Johannine love discourse (Joh 13-17 beyond export); Synoptic love-commandment traditions. Synthesis conclusions about NT love theology should be held provisional pending more complete verse coverage.

operations.description: Phase 2 flag: Song of Songs as erotic theology

record.description: Song 8:6 — love is strong as death... the very flame of the LORD — is the OT's nearest approach to a theology of love's ultimate nature. A Phase 2 study of the Song as a whole is needed: its erotic vocabulary within the love architecture, its allegorical tradition (divine-human love as referent), and its canonical significance as the inclusion of embodied desire within the sacred literature. Required before Session D synthesis of the love-eros-agape relationship.

operations.description: Update registry record to reflect Session B completion

set.notes:
123 terms (120 extracted). 3 excluded. 3 parts. Session A Instruction v7. MTI: extracted.
[AUDIT 2026-03-24] result=REVIEW  terms=141  verses=1001  run_id=RUN-20260324_171017-AUDIT_WORD
[SESSION B 2026-03-24] Session B v3.0 complete. Analysis document: WA-103-love-analysis-2026-03-24.md. 10 MTI terms newly assigned (extracted/xref). ~55 bleed terms assigned delete. 6 Phase 2 flags raised (PH2-103-001 through PH2-103-006). Key findings: love spans all four concentric circles simultaneously; *God is love* is the programme's most radical theological claim; love-fear structural opposition (1Jo 4:18) is a Session D priority; the primary love verb (H0157G) remains in desire registry. Session D priority: Wave 2 — potential integrating principle for the entire inner-being architecture.

_patch_summary.note: H0157G and H0157H assigned xref_desire status. H7349 rachum and H7453 rea are the most analytically significant new extractions — essential for the compassion dimension and the love-your-neighbour commandment respectively.
